title: Fix Ctrl Alt key-hint merging in the Zellij bar
description:
Context from investigation on June 25, 2026:

The reported screenshots were from yazelix-next, not main Yazelix. The bottom Zellij status-bar key hints visually merge with a shared modifier prefix when using simple Ctrl bindings, but with Ctrl Alt bindings the row expands into separate full chords such as `Ctrl Alt g > LOCK`, `Ctrl Alt p > PANE`, `Ctrl Alt t > TAB`, etc. The desired behavior is the compact merged shape, without renaming tabs or encoding tab decorations into tab names.

Relevant user-visible symptom:
- Ctrl-only mode hints render compactly as a shared prefix plus per-action keys.
- Ctrl Alt mode hints render each action as a separate full chord, wasting width and making the bar visually noisy.
- Separate related concern: activity dots can sometimes remain forever until the user focuses the tab, even after the pane that generated the activity has stopped. Track/verify this if the chosen bar implementation touches tab activity indicators.

Sources checked:
- Zellij modifier documentation: https://zellij.dev/documentation/changing-modifiers.html
- Zellij key-tooltips announcement/reference: https://zellij.dev/news/web-client-multiple-pane-actions/#key-tooltips
- zjstatus-hints plugin: https://github.com/b0o/zjstatus-hints

Findings:
- Zellij supports changing primary/secondary modifiers, including multi-modifier chords such as Ctrl Alt, when the terminal can report them. The official docs do not expose a setting for status-bar hint joining/formatting.
- This looks like a rendering issue in the built-in Zellij one-line status-bar hint renderer, not a keybind parsing issue.
- In the Zellij status-bar source, the one-line renderer computes common modifiers across all mode-indicator shortcuts, not only the row/mode currently being rendered. That means Ctrl-only can be extracted as a common prefix, while Ctrl Alt can fail to merge if another mode's exit/toggle key lacks Alt.
- Relevant upstream/fork source paths from the earlier inspection:
  - `default-plugins/status-bar/src/one_line_ui.rs`
  - `default-plugins/status-bar/src/first_line.rs`
  - `default-plugins/status-bar/src/main.rs`
- In `one_line_ui.rs`, the relevant flow was:
  - `render_mode_key_indicators(...)`
  - `common_modifiers_in_all_modes(&default_keys)`
  - `render_common_modifiers(...)`
  - fallback through full per-shortcut rendering when no common modifier survives
- Likely small fix: compute common modifiers only for the currently rendered mode's visible shortcuts, then render one shared prefix such as `Ctrl-Alt +` and stripped per-action keys. Add a regression test where Normal-mode indicators all use Ctrl Alt, while a non-current mode has only Ctrl or a different exit shortcut; the rendered Normal row should still use the shared Ctrl Alt prefix.

Alternative path:
- `zjstatus-hints` can feed Zellij keybinding hints into `zjstatus` through a named pipe and may avoid the built-in status-bar renderer entirely.
- That route is larger: it adds plugin/pipe/vendoring/permission/update-path complexity and may have limited formatting configurability. It is worth considering only if yazelix-next chooses a zjstatus-owned bar surface rather than a small Zellij fork fix.

Decision pressure:
- Do not solve this by renaming tabs.
- Prefer a clean ownership choice: either fix the Zellij fork's status-bar renderer, or intentionally replace the built-in bar with a zjstatus/zjstatus-hints style surface.
- For the smallest yazelix-next slice, first evaluate a focused Zellij fork patch plus a regression test before rebuilding the bar from first principles.

Acceptance criteria:
- Ctrl Alt default hints in yazelix-next render with a shared modifier prefix instead of repeating full chords per action.
- The fix does not require changing tab names.
- If the implementation affects tab activity markers, verify that stale activity dots clear without requiring focus on the tab.
- The chosen ownership boundary is recorded: Zellij fork renderer fix, zjstatus-hints integration, or first-party replacement.
notes: Runtime correction on June 25, 2026: the source fix at yazelix-zellij 5769da50 was not enough because Zellij embeds the status-bar plugin WASM from zellij-utils/assets/plugins/status-bar.wasm. Regenerated and pushed the embedded asset in yazelix-zellij 10c79f7e, then updated yazelix-next flake.lock to 10c79f7e and rebuilt .#yzn from the normal locked flake.
close_reason: Completed: fixed in yazelix-zellij commit 5769da50 by deriving one-line mode-hint common modifiers from the currently visible row instead of all modes. Added status-bar regression coverage for Ctrl-Alt hints with another mode using Ctrl. Pushed the fork, updated yazelix-next flake.lock to 5769da50, verified cargo test -p status-bar, nix build .#yzn with override, normal nix build .#yzn, contracts check, flake show --all-systems, and refreshed installed yzn.

title: Support grouped modifier clusters in the Zellij bar
description: The one-line Zellij status bar currently supports at most one shared modifier prefix for the visible mode-hint row. If high-frequency/default Zellij keys such as Ctrl p, Ctrl t, and Ctrl q stay on plain Ctrl while the rest use Ctrl Alt, the renderer should show readable modifier clusters instead of degrading into one noisy mixed row. Keep the key-remap decision separate unless the selected slice explicitly pairs this with the Ctrl p/t/q remap.
design: Code context from yazelix-zellij/default-plugins/status-bar/src/one_line_ui.rs: render_mode_key_indicators currently has exactly two paths: one shared modifier prefix for the whole visible mode-hint row, or no shared prefix. common_modifiers_for_shortcuts now derives that one prefix from the current row for yazelix-next-1mb, which fixes all-Ctrl-Alt rows but cannot cleanly display mixed Ctrl and Ctrl-Alt rows. add_shortcut_with_inline_key and add_shortcut_with_key_only already render stripped keys after a shared prefix, so grouped rendering can reuse them. Better target shape: group rendered hints by modifier set, with a stable group order of Ctrl, Ctrl Alt, then Alt. Within each group, preserve the relative action order from the source row. For a Ctrl p/t/q keymap, the mode row should render as a Ctrl group for PANE/TAB/QUIT and a Ctrl Alt group for LOCK/RESIZE/SEARCH/SESSION. Alt is reserved for yzn-level hints such as Alt m and Alt Shift h if those are brought into the same surface.
acceptance_criteria: Mixed modifier rows render readable modifier groups: Ctrl, Ctrl Alt, and Alt where present; within each group, action order is stable; regression coverage includes Ctrl p/t/q plus Ctrl Alt for the remaining normal-mode hints; existing all-Ctrl-Alt single-prefix behavior from yazelix-next-1mb still passes; no tab-name hacks or zjstatus replacement.
notes: Recommendation: worth doing if we choose Ctrl p/t/q. The status bar should group by modifier set rather than preserve the original interleaved row literally. That keeps the visual model simple: Ctrl, Ctrl Alt, Alt.
close_reason: Completed: grouped modifier clusters and compact secondary hints shipped through child commit addea0e9 and parent commit f7a43ab. Manual dogfooding confirms the status bar is good.

title: Add shared config CLI and ratconfig core
description:
## Why

The public swap gate chooses both CLI config support and ratconfig UI, but only through one shared config core. Yazelix Next needs a small semantic config owner before it can provide a real config UI popup or user-editable Yazelix-owned settings without scattering config logic across CLI, UI, native files, and runtime helpers.

## User-Visible Behavior

A user and an agent can inspect and change supported Yazelix-owned settings through a stable command surface, and the same settings can later be edited through a ratconfig UI popup. Native config files remain directly editable for Mars, Starship, Nushell, guarded Zellij, and Yazi where those surfaces exist.

## Decision Context

Public swap gate: `yazelix-next-public-swap-gate-o4i`.

Recorded direction:
- CLI is the non-negotiable base because it is scriptable, testable, agent-friendly, useful for Home Manager/debugging, and easy to cover with contracts.
- Ratconfig is the human-facing layer because it makes config discoverable inside Yazelix, especially from a popup.
- Do not build separate CLI and UI logic.
- Preferred ownership is one schema, one validation path, one migration/contract path, and two frontends.
- Ratconfig is the config platform, not the config policy engine.

## Scope

In scope:
- Define the smallest semantic Yazelix Next config contract before coding.
- Add a shared Rust config core that owns schema, validation, defaults, reads, writes, and contract/migration use.
- Add minimal CLI commands for supported semantic settings, likely `get`, `set`, `unset`, `path`, and `open`, but keep the final command list small.
- Integrate ratconfig as the shared editor/patch/contract library only where it reduces code and does not take over Yazelix policy.
- Choose the initial storage file and format deliberately. Prefer a single Yazelix-owned semantic config file under `~/.config/yazelix-next/`.
- Keep native config files as native surfaces, not translated generic config blobs.
- Expose enough model/data for a future ratconfig UI popup to reuse the same core.
- Add focused contract checks for defaults, get/set/unset, validation failures, and contract/migration behavior if migrations exist.
- Update `README.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, and the LOC scorecard.

Out of scope:
- Do not implement the full ratconfig TUI popup in this bead unless the user explicitly chooses to combine it.
- Do not build a broad settings surface. Start with a tiny set of real semantic settings.
- Do not manage Mars, Starship, Nushell, Zellij, or Yazi native config semantics through this core by default.
- Do not implement a generic format translator between native config files.
- Do not port main Yazelix's full `settings.jsonc` contract or Home Manager config machinery.
- Do not add popup launching here except for the command that a future popup will run.

## Config Surface Tiers

1. Semantic Yazelix config: edited by CLI and ratconfig UI, backed by ratconfig contracts/migrations. Examples include bar widgets, popup commands, sidebar width, and selected keybinding choices.
2. Native config surfaces: Mars TOML, Starship TOML, Nushell files, guarded Zellij KDL, and Yazi files. Users can edit these directly. Ratconfig may show status and open them, but should not blindly own their semantics.
3. Guarded integration config: Zellij and Yazi can break the runtime spine, so Yazelix Next must keep validators and ownership boundaries. Ratconfig can render safe semantic fields and diagnostics, but must not become a generic native merge engine.

## Implementation Notes

1. Start with one or two real settings only. Good candidates are settings already chosen as swap gates, such as popup table fields or bar widget selection, but avoid creating a config setting before the runtime owner exists.
2. Prefer TOML or JSONC based on ratconfig support and user ergonomics, not main Yazelix compatibility. If JSONC is chosen, justify why comments matter more than TOML simplicity.
3. The CLI should be deterministic and check-friendly. Avoid interactive prompts in the base commands.
4. Ratconfig can own comment-preserving patches, deterministic contracts, field model helpers, diagnostics, and later UI rendering.
5. Yazelix Next owns the schema, meaning of fields, runtime apply policy, safety guards, and native config boundaries.

## Verification

- CLI contract check covers reading defaults when no config exists.
- CLI contract check covers setting and unsetting a supported value.
- CLI contract check covers rejecting an unsupported path or invalid value clearly.
- If contract state/migration is introduced, a check covers joined state and one deterministic reconcile path.
- `nix flake check`.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` if the runtime package changes.
- Temp profile install if the package surface changes.
- `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` after runtime changes.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the first slice requires a broad settings schema.
- Stop if ratconfig starts owning Yazelix runtime policy instead of generic config mechanics.
- Stop if native config handling becomes a generic merge/translation system.
- Stop if CLI and future UI need separate validation or write paths.

title: Adopt standalone Yazelix bar foundation
description:
## Why

Yazelix Next should eventually consume `yazelix-zellij-bar` because the bar is core to the Yazelix experience, but the first slice should be the standalone generic bar foundation rather than the full main-Yazelix status system. This keeps the Next architecture small while giving future widgets a stable visual and packaging base.

## Evidence

- `ARCHITECTURE.md:3`: Yazelix Next is currently a small Nix/Lix flake with one user-facing command, `yzn`.
- `ARCHITECTURE.md:23`: `config.kdl`, `layout.kdl`, and `layout.swap.kdl` currently own Zellij behavior and layout.
- `layout.kdl:2`: the current top bar is native `plugin location="tab-bar"`.
- `layout.kdl:6`: the current bottom bar is native `plugin location="status-bar"` and should keep owning key hints for this slice.
- `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix/docs/contracts/standalone_yazelix_zellij_bar_distribution.md:5`: `yazelix_zellij_bar` is the standalone package for the Yazelix-branded Zellij status-bar preset.
- `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix/docs/contracts/standalone_yazelix_zellij_bar_distribution.md:30`: the standalone bar has no central config framework; KDL is the public surface.
- `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix/docs/contracts/standalone_yazelix_zellij_bar_distribution.md:45`: the generic default must not require `yzx`, `yzx_control`, pane orchestrator, Yazelix caches, Nushell, tokenusage, or full Yazelix.
- `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix/docs/contracts/status_bar_ownership.md:5`: full Yazelix status-bar ownership is split across zjstatus, the child bar package, runtime adapters, and the pane orchestrator, which is too much scope for the first Next slice.

## Scope

In scope:
- Add `github:luccahuguet/yazelix-zellij-bar` as an external flake input.
- Consume the child package as a packaged artifact, not copied Rust/KDL source.
- Replace the native top `tab-bar` pane in `layout.kdl` templates with the child standalone/generic `zjstatus` plugin block.
- Keep the native bottom `status-bar` for Zellij key hints.
- Add a focused contract check that the packaged layout uses the child bar plugin and still contains the native bottom status bar.
- Update `README.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, and the LOC scorecard for the new runtime behavior.
- Refresh the installed `yzn` runtime after the flake changes.

Out of scope:
- Do not adopt `render-yazelix-runtime` yet.
- Do not add workspace, activity, sidebar/editor fact, provider usage, CPU, RAM, version, or custom command widgets in this slice.
- Do not add ratconfig, a bar settings file, a bar CLI surface, Home Manager options, or generated user config.
- Do not copy `yazelix-zellij-bar` Rust or preset files into this repo unless package consumption is impossible.
- Do not replace the bottom native Zellij `status-bar` in this slice.

## Implementation Notes

1. Add the `yazelix-zellij-bar` flake input and make it follow this repo's `nixpkgs` when practical.
2. Reference `yazelixZellijBar.packages.${system}.yazelix_zellij_bar` from `flake.nix`.
3. Materialize a layout-owned top bar plugin block from the child package output, preserving this repo's `@yazi@` substitutions and existing swap layout behavior.
4. Keep the bottom `plugin location="status-bar"` unchanged so the compact modifier-hint work remains visible.
5. Extend the existing layout/config contract check or add a small Rust check only if needed; prefer validating observable packaged layout properties over brittle string trivia.
6. Dogfood manually only after the build checks pass, because visual correctness is the real final signal for this slice.

## Verification

- `nix flake check` should exit 0 and include the bar/layout contract check.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0 and package the layout with the standalone Yazelix bar at the top and native status bar at the bottom.
- `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>` should exit 0 for a clean profile install.
- `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` should refresh the user's installed runtime after the package changes.
- Manual `yzn` dogfooding should show the Yazelix-branded top bar while preserving bottom key hints and the existing Yazi/sidebar layout behavior.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if `yazelix-zellij-bar` cannot be consumed as a package artifact without copying child source into this repo.
- Stop if the implementation requires adopting workspace/activity/provider widgets to get a usable top bar.
- Stop if replacing the top bar breaks sidebar swap layout behavior or bottom key-hint visibility.
- Stop if the child bar package contract has drifted materially from the cited standalone/generic boundary.

## Maintenance Notes

- This is a foundation bead. Add widgets later only when each widget has a clear owner, contract, and value.
- The preferred order after this slice is cheap standalone widgets first, provider widgets later, and Yazelix-specific workspace/activity facts last.
- The rejected direction is importing the full main-Yazelix integrated status system into Next before Next has a real status/event owner.

title: Add a runtime contract matrix
description:
## Why

The architecture doc names owners and tradeoffs, but it does not yet give future agents a compact matrix of runtime contracts and their checks. A matrix would make it obvious which surfaces are protected, manually dogfooded, or still unchecked.

## Evidence

- `ARCHITECTURE.md:14-43`: names owners for flake, Mars, Zellij, Nushell, Yazi, yzn-open, and layout checks.
- `ARCHITECTURE.md:80-85`: calls out hard dependencies and integration-heavy Yazi behavior as current risks.
- `AGENTS.md:15-20`: asks agents to state behavior, choose one owner, and add focused validation.

## Scope

In scope:
- `ARCHITECTURE.md`
- Possibly `README.md` if a short pointer is useful.

Out of scope:
- Implementing missing checks.
- Changing runtime behavior.
- Adding broad compatibility policies.

## Implementation Notes

1. Add a table with columns like Contract, Owner, Check, Manual dogfood, Missing coverage.
2. Cover at least: `yzn` launch, Mars config, Zellij layout/swap, Nushell layering, Yazi opener, Helix bridge, desktop entry, session/window isolation.
3. Mark unchecked contracts plainly rather than pretending they are covered.
4. Link unchecked items to existing Beads where one exists, such as session/window isolation or Yazi config management.
5. Keep the table compact; it should help agents choose validation, not become a design document dump.

## Verification

- `git diff --check` should exit 0.
- `wc -l .gitignore AGENTS.md README.md ARCHITECTURE.md flake.nix mars.toml config.kdl layout.kdl layout.swap.kdl nu/config.nu nu/env.nu helix/config.toml yazi/init.lua yazi/plugins/sidebar-status.yazi/main.lua yazi/yazi.toml crates/yzn-open/Cargo.toml crates/yzn-open/src/main.rs checks/zellij-layout.rs runtime/yzn-nu.rs` should match the README scorecard if docs change.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the matrix starts prescribing new features instead of documenting current contracts.
- Stop if the table cannot be verified from source and existing Beads.

## Maintenance Notes

The matrix should make the agent path obvious: contract first, existing owner second, cheapest check third.
close_reason: Completed: ARCHITECTURE.md includes a compact runtime contract matrix covering yzn launch, Mars config, Zellij layout/swap, keybindings, Nushell layering, Yazi opener, Helix bridge, desktop entry, checks, and missing coverage. LOC scorecard updated.

title: Add lightweight CHANGELOG.md
description:
Add a small CHANGELOG.md so user-visible runtime changes are recorded outside chat history.

Acceptance criteria:
- Create a lightweight CHANGELOG.md format that fits the repo's minimal architecture, likely an Unreleased section plus dated release sections only when needed.
- Capture current major user-visible slices at a high level without turning it into release bureaucracy.
- Add a short maintainer rule only if needed: update the changelog when behavior, commands, keymaps, packaged tools, or runtime contracts change.
- Update the README LOC scorecard if project files change.

Rejected direction: do not add release automation, generated changelogs, or versioning machinery until the user chooses that scope.
close_reason: Completed: added lightweight CHANGELOG.md, documented the maintainer update rule, refreshed the README LOC scorecard, and verified the docs diff and LOC totals.

title: Adopt contract-driven check-backed development
description:
## Why

Yazelix Next is fully agent-driven, so project intent must live in durable repo state rather than chat history. The current repo already points in this direction, but the method is not yet explicit enough for future agents.

## Evidence

- `AGENTS.md:13-20`: requires contract distillation, one repo owner, focused validation, and recording rejected alternatives in Beads.
- `AGENTS.md:48-49`: says important decisions should be kept in Beads instead of chat history.
- `ARCHITECTURE.md:89-94`: says the main simplicity win is one owner per contract and no duplicated compatibility surface.
- `README.md:59-73`: the repo tracks owned LOC, so process changes should stay visible and justified.

## Scope

In scope:
- Track the small implementation program for adopting contract-driven, check-backed development.
- Keep the method compatible with AI-agent-first development.

Out of scope:
- Implement feature work.
- Add broad process machinery or templates that create more overhead than clarity.

## Implementation Notes

1. Complete the child beads that document the method, expose reusable checks, and map current contracts to checks.
2. Close this epic when those child beads are done or explicitly rejected.

## Verification

- `br dep tree $EPIC` should show the child work.
- `br ready` should keep implementation beads independently actionable.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the user chooses a different development method.
- Stop if a child bead would require implementing unrelated runtime features.

## Maintenance Notes

The intended method is contract-driven and check-backed. Use TDD only where it is the cheapest way to prove a small deterministic helper or regression.
close_reason: Completed: child beads documented the workflow, added flake contract checks, and added the architecture contract matrix.

title: Decide yzn key remaps
description:
Decide the small first-class key remap set for yzn before adding more bindings. Current owned Zellij config remaps Alt n to Alt m for new stacked panes and uses Alt Shift h to show/hide the sidebar.

Acceptance criteria:
- List the remaps yzn should own, including current Alt m and Alt Shift h behavior.
- Compare against main Yazelix muscle memory, Zellij defaults, Mars/window-manager conflicts, and Yazi/Helix/Nushell keys.
- Mark each candidate as keep, add, change, or reject, with a short reason.
- Choose one owner for each accepted remap, normally config.kdl unless another layer is clearly better.
- Do not implement remaps in this bead unless the user explicitly chooses the final set.
close_reason: Decided and implemented the first yzn key policy: Ctrl Alt owns native Zellij mode control for lock/pane/tab/resize/search/session/quit, Ctrl Alt h/j/k/l owns direct tab/pane movement, Alt m remains new stacked pane, Alt Shift h remains sidebar toggle, and Move mode is intentionally unbound. Packaged config is now covered by yzn-contracts.

title: Document the contract-driven agent workflow
description:
## Why

The repo has the seeds of the workflow, but future agents need the final method written in current-state project instructions: contract first, one owner, smallest implementation, focused check, rejected alternatives in Beads. This prevents generic TDD, broad compatibility surfaces, and mechanical ports from main Yazelix.

## Evidence

- `AGENTS.md:13-20`: contract distillation exists but does not name the final method or explain when TDD is appropriate.
- `AGENTS.md:61-68`: verification guidance exists but is command-oriented, not tied to feature contracts.
- `ARCHITECTURE.md:89-94`: architecture doc already frames one owner per contract as the real simplicity win.

## Scope

In scope:
- `AGENTS.md`
- `ARCHITECTURE.md`
- A short README pointer only if it improves discoverability.

Out of scope:
- Source code changes.
- New feature work.
- A heavyweight process template file.

## Implementation Notes

1. Add a concise section naming the method: contract-driven, check-backed development.
2. Define the loop: contract, owner, check, implementation, verification, rejected alternatives.
3. State when TDD is useful: Rust helpers, parsers, regressions, deterministic CLI behavior.
4. State when TDD is not the default: layout design, runtime integration, fork decisions, dogfooding surfaces.
5. Keep wording terse enough for agents to follow quickly.

## Verification

- `git diff --check` should exit 0.
- `wc -l .gitignore AGENTS.md README.md ARCHITECTURE.md flake.nix mars.toml config.kdl layout.kdl layout.swap.kdl nu/config.nu nu/env.nu helix/config.toml yazi/init.lua yazi/plugins/sidebar-status.yazi/main.lua yazi/yazi.toml crates/yzn-open/Cargo.toml crates/yzn-open/src/main.rs checks/zellij-layout.rs runtime/yzn-nu.rs` should match the README scorecard if docs change.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if documenting the method would require choosing new product scope.
- Stop if the docs become a generic process essay instead of actionable agent guidance.

## Maintenance Notes

Do not call the default process TDD. The stronger name is contract-driven, check-backed development.
close_reason: Completed: AGENTS.md names contract-driven, check-backed development; states when TDD fits and when it is not the default; verification guidance includes nix flake check. Verified git diff --check, LOC scorecard, nix flake show, nix flake check, nix build, temp profile install, and profile refresh.

title: Expose flake checks for runtime contracts
description:
## Why

Agent-driven development needs one deterministic command that proves the core runtime contracts without launching a GUI. The repo currently has packages/apps and build-time guards, but no `checks` output that an agent can run as the default contract suite.

## Evidence

- `AGENTS.md:61-68`: runtime changes normally require `nix flake show --all-systems`, `nix build .#yzn`, and a profile install check.
- `flake.nix:39-44`: local Rust helpers are compiled inside Nix.
- `flake.nix:145-149`: `yzn-layout-check` validates the Zellij layout during the package build.
- `runtime/yzn-nu.rs:24-49`: `yzn-nu` owns generated Nushell config layering and exec behavior, but its smoke test is manual.

## Scope

In scope:
- `flake.nix`
- `checks/` or small Nix check derivations
- Existing Rust helpers if exposing checks requires tiny adjustments.

Out of scope:
- GUI launch tests.
- Home Manager or desktop environment test infrastructure.
- Broad CI setup unless the user separately chooses it.

## Implementation Notes

1. Add `checks.${system}` outputs for cheap runtime contracts.
2. Include at least: package build, Zellij layout template/swap validation, and `yzn-nu` config layering with a temporary config root.
3. Keep checks non-interactive and independent from the user's real home/config.
4. Prefer Nix derivations that execute the exact built artifacts over separate mock implementations.
5. Document the chosen command in `AGENTS.md` or README if it becomes the recommended default.

## Verification

- `nix flake check` should exit 0 on the current system.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should still exit 0.
- `git diff --check` should exit 0.
- README LOC scorecard should be updated if owned files change.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if a check requires opening Mars or a graphical Zellij session.
- Stop if the check duplicates large runtime behavior instead of executing the built helper.
- Stop if `nix flake check` becomes so heavy that agents avoid running it.

## Maintenance Notes

This bead is about agent affordance: one obvious command should prove the contracts that currently require remembering ad hoc commands from chat.
close_reason: Completed: flake.nix exposes checks for yzn and contracts. nix flake check builds yzn and runs a non-GUI yzn-nu temporary-config smoke test that proves packaged config, user layering, carapace, and zoxide. Verified nix flake check and nix build .#yzn.

title: Start Herdr-inspired yzn session attach flow
description:
## Why

The Herdr research bead proved the useful direction but intentionally stopped before implementation. The first practical Yazelix Next step is not to build a multiplexer or adopt Mars Next live-session ownership. It is to make `yzn` consume Zellij's existing session primitives so closing a window or starting another `yzn` can attach to the right runtime state without breaking the Yazi-to-Helix bridge.

## User-Visible Behavior To Distill

A user should have an obvious way to reopen the same Yazelix Next session after detach/close, while existing panes keep their identity and file opens from Yazi continue targeting the correct Helix bridge. Creating a fresh independent session should remain possible, but it must be explicit enough that bridge/session state does not leak between windows.

## Source Context

- Herdr research: `yazelix-next-t1w`.
- Herdr model: background server owns live PTYs; clients attach/detach; session JSON stores shape, not arbitrary process handles; live handoff is deferred as too expensive.
- Current Yazelix Next launch: `flake.nix` launches Mars then Zellij with `--new-session-with-layout`, which biases every `yzn` start toward a fresh generated Zellij session.
- Current bridge isolation: `YAZELIX_HELIX_BRIDGE_SESSION_ID` is top-level-launch-scoped, and `yzn-open` also checks `ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME` when available.
- Zellij primitives: named sessions, `attach --create`, list sessions, kill/delete sessions, background creation, `on_force_close detach`, and session serialization.
- Mars Next context: `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/mars-next` has a single-terminal `mars-next-live-session` PTY boundary, but it has no multiplexer server/client model, no detach/attach, no workspace/tab model, and no socket API. It is future terminal-core evidence, not the current yzn session owner.

## Scope

In scope:
- Define the first `yzn` session contract before coding: what plain `yzn` means, how a fresh session is requested, and which command surface is intentionally absent.
- Prefer a tiny surface around Zellij sessions, likely stable default attach/create plus one explicit fresh-session path if needed.
- Preserve managed Mars launch, packaged Zellij config, packaged layout, Yazi sidebar, Helix bridge, and current helper binaries.
- Make bridge identity session-scoped enough that attaching to an existing Zellij session does not mint a conflicting `YAZELIX_HELIX_BRIDGE_SESSION_ID` for already-running panes.
- Apply the managed layout only when creating or resurrecting a session, not blindly on every attach.
- Add the cheapest check that proves command construction and bridge/session identity behavior without launching a GUI.
- Update `README.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, and the LOC scorecard if runtime behavior changes.
- Refresh the installed `yzn` runtime after changing the flake runtime.

Out of scope:
- Do not build a background Yazelix daemon.
- Do not own PTYs, transfer file descriptors, or implement Herdr live handoff.
- Do not consume `mars-next-live-session` for this slice.
- Do not add agent sidebar/state detection yet.
- Do not add broad CLI session management if a smaller attach/create contract is enough.
- Do not expose Zellij-native config sidecar or bar work in this bead.

## Implementation Notes

1. First inspect the packaged Yazelix Zellij CLI behavior for `attach --create`, `--layout`, `--new-session-with-layout`, `--session`, `session_name`, and `attach_to_session` options without launching a GUI.
2. Decide whether the smallest safe launch shape is CLI-driven (`zellij attach --create <name> --layout <layout> ...`) or config/layout-driven (`session_name`, `attach_to_session`, `default_layout`).
3. Keep the session name stable and Yazelix-owned. A likely first default is a single named session such as `yzn`, but this must be checked against existing generated-name/title behavior and multi-window expectations.
4. If supporting fresh independent sessions, prefer an explicit command or flag rather than changing plain `yzn` ambiguity.
5. Derive `YAZELIX_HELIX_BRIDGE_SESSION_ID` from the durable Zellij session name or another stable session-scoped value for attached sessions. Do not use a new random top-level client id when attaching to existing panes.
6. Extend Rust contract checks if command assembly moves into Rust. If the launcher stays shell/Nix, add a focused check that validates the packaged launcher text/argv and bridge id rules without running Mars or Zellij interactively.
7. Manual dogfooding is required before claiming UX correctness, but automated checks should catch the identity/layout regression class first.

## Verification

- Focused contract check for the new launch/session command shape.
- Focused bridge/session identity check proving attach does not create a conflicting helper id.
- `nix flake check`.
- `nix flake show --all-systems`.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs`.
- Temp profile install with `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>`.
- `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` after runtime changes.
- Manual dogfooding: create session, detach/close, reopen/attach, open from Yazi into the existing Helix bridge, then create a fresh independent session if that surface is implemented.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if Zellij cannot attach/create with the managed layout without creating duplicate startup panes.
- Stop if bridge identity cannot be made session-scoped without breaking standalone helper behavior.
- Stop if the smallest implementation starts becoming a session manager framework.
- Stop if user-visible semantics for plain `yzn` versus fresh session are unclear; ask for the decision before coding.

## Rejected For This Slice

- Herdr-style PTY master ownership and SCM_RIGHTS handoff.
- A Yazelix background daemon or socket API.
- Mars Next live-session as the yzn session owner.
- Agent-state/sidebar work before session attach/detach is correct.

Correction: the previous CLI/terminal-support comment on this bead was corrupted by shell interpolation of backticked command names. Treat this comment as authoritative.

CLI and terminal support decision:

- `yzx`, `yzx help`, `yzx -h`, and `yzx --help` show help.
- `yzx enter` enters Yazelix inside the current terminal. This is the terminal-agnostic path for Kitty, Alacritty, Foot, Ghostty, WezTerm, and other user-preferred terminals.
- `yzx launch` launches the curated Mars Yazelix app. The desktop entry should call this.
- `yzx attach` is reserved for a future explicit attach/session picker.

Mental model:

- `launch` = bring your own Yazelix window.
- `enter` = bring your own terminal.
- `attach` = bring your own existing session.

Terminal support policy:

Ship Mars as the owned/default experience, but support mainstream terminals through documentation that teaches users to configure their terminal's initial command to `yzx enter`. Yazelix Next should not own Kitty/Alacritty/Foot/Ghostty/WezTerm native configs, themes, fonts, opacity, cursor effects, or per-terminal generated materialization. Once the terminal starts `yzx enter`, Yazelix owns the managed runtime.

Implementation implication:

This bead should treat `yzx enter` as the first important session command. It must attach/create predictably and preserve Yazi-to-Helix bridge isolation. `yzx launch` can wrap Mars around the same runtime entry path. `yzx attach` should remain future scope unless the user explicitly pulls it in.

title: Design minimal Yazi config management
description:
## Context

Yazelix Next now uses Yazi as the left file-manager sidebar. We want users to be happy with real file-manager behavior: copy/cut/paste files and folders, previews, zoxide/fzf, search, open-with behavior, visual selection, bulk operations, themes/icons, and normal Yazi ergonomics. That makes Yazi the pragmatic file-manager choice for now.

The risk is config management. Main Yazelix handles Yazi config through a full managed materialization system: Rust adapter/writer, generated runtime config, TOML/keymap merging, user init appending, managed plugin/flavor import, legacy path rejection, and a separate yazi-assets renderer. Current measured related surfaces were roughly 2.5k LOC in main Yazelix Yazi materialization/docs/plugins, 1.1k LOC in shared import/path/stub support, and 8k LOC in yazelix-yazi-assets. Do not recreate that shape in Yazelix Next without a very strong reason.

## Direction

Keep the boundary simple:

- Yazi owns file management.
- Yazelix Next owns workspace integration.
- Packaged Yazi config owns only the baseline behavior needed for the sidebar to feel good out of the box.
- User customization should be added only when there is a concrete workflow need, not as a generic config-management project.

Preferred future shape, if/when needed:

1. Keep repo-owned packaged Yazi config tiny and explicit.
2. Avoid TOML deep-merge and generated config directories unless forced by a real use case.
3. Prefer narrow extension points over a broad managed Yazi home.
4. If layering is needed, start with the smallest safe surface, probably Lua `init.lua` after packaged setup, then reassess.
5. Record any irreversible ownership decisions before implementation.

## Rejected Or Deferred Alternatives

- Fork Yazi to remove glue: rejected as a default. It inherits upstream churn and still needs Yazelix integration policy.
- Fork Elio now: deferred. Elio is worth evaluating, but forking before proving it is simpler than a tiny bridge is premature.
- Roll our own terminal file manager: rejected for the user-happiness target because expected file-manager features are broad.
- Zellij-plugin file manager: deferred as a long-term idea; if pursued, keep the core independent from Zellij with a Zellij adapter, but do not replace Yazi now.
- Bite the bullet and port main Yazelix materialization: rejected for Yazelix Next simplicity unless future requirements clearly justify it.

## Acceptance Criteria For A Future Implementation

- State the exact user-visible workflow that requires config management.
- Prove why packaged defaults are not enough.
- Add the smallest config surface that solves that workflow.
- Keep the README LOC scorecard honest.
- Do not introduce generated config directories, TOML merging, plugin import, migration logic, or compatibility shims unless each has a concrete acceptance criterion.
- Keep runtime validation focused on observable Yazi behavior, not generic config plumbing.

title: Add guarded Zellij native config sidecar
description:
## Why

Zellij preferences should be user-editable, but a full native Zellij config replacement would let user config break the Yazelix Next runtime spine. The current packaged config owns the managed shell, key remaps, pane creation key, and sidebar toggle, so the first user config surface must be a guarded native sidecar for safe preferences only.

## Evidence

- `flake.nix:83`: the packaged Zellij config is generated from repo-owned `config.kdl` with the managed Nushell path substituted in.
- `flake.nix:187`: `yzn` launches Zellij with `--config ${yznConfigKdl}` and `--new-session-with-layout ${yznZellijLayout}/layout.kdl`.
- `config.kdl:1`: `default_shell` is managed and points at the packaged `yzn-nu` shell.
- `config.kdl:3`: `keybinds` is managed by Yazelix Next.
- `config.kdl:31`: `Alt m` is the managed new-pane binding used with the layout behavior.
- `config.kdl:32`: `Alt Shift h` is the managed sidebar swap binding.
- `layout.kdl:1`: layout ownership is separate from config and currently defines the managed tab/sidebar/status-bar structure.
- `ARCHITECTURE.md:23`: `config.kdl`, `layout.kdl`, and `layout.swap.kdl` are documented Zellij behavior owners.

## Scope

In scope:
- Add an optional guarded sidecar at `~/.config/yazelix-next/zellij/config.kdl`.
- Build the runtime Zellij config from the packaged `config.kdl` plus the user sidecar only when the sidecar passes validation.
- Treat the sidecar as native Zellij preferences, not a full Zellij config replacement.
- Fail fast with a clear message when the sidecar contains dangerous top-level nodes or settings that would replace Yazelix-owned behavior.
- Add a focused contract check for accepted safe sidecar content and rejected dangerous content.
- Document the sidecar path, allowed intent, and forbidden ownership boundaries in `README.md` and `ARCHITECTURE.md`.
- Update `CHANGELOG.md` and the README LOC scorecard.
- Refresh the installed `yzn` runtime after changing the flake runtime.

Out of scope:
- Do not allow user replacement of `config.kdl`, `layout.kdl`, or `layout.swap.kdl`.
- Do not support arbitrary native Zellij keymaps in this sidecar.
- Do not support layout ownership, plugin alias ownership, default shell ownership, default layout ownership, session startup ownership, or pane policy ownership through this sidecar.
- Do not add semantic key remaps, profiles, ratconfig, Home Manager, or a CLI config editor in this slice.
- Do not silently ignore dangerous config. Reject it so the user knows why the sidecar is not being used.

## Implementation Notes

1. Define the allowed/forbidden sidecar contract before coding. Start strict and widen later only with concrete user value.
2. Prefer a small Rust validator if text checks would be brittle. The validator does not need a complete KDL semantic model; it only needs to reject forbidden top-level ownership nodes/settings reliably enough for the current contract.
3. Initial forbidden top-level items should include at least `keybinds`, `default_shell`, `default_layout`, `layout`, `plugins`, `load_plugins`, and any other node that clearly takes ownership of startup, layout, shell, plugin loading, or key policy.
4. Generate a runtime config under `YAZELIX_STATE_DIR` or another launch-local runtime directory by concatenating packaged config and validated sidecar content.
5. Launch Zellij with the generated runtime config path when a sidecar exists; otherwise keep using the packaged config path.
6. Keep layout and swap layout packaging unchanged.

## Verification

- `nix flake check` should exit 0 and include sidecar validation contract coverage.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0 and package the sidecar-aware launcher/config helper.
- `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>` should exit 0 for a clean profile install.
- `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` should refresh the user's installed runtime.
- Manual dogfooding, when requested, should show safe sidecar preferences applying without losing the managed shell, `Alt m`, `Alt Shift h`, sidebar layout, or bottom status-bar key hints.
- A sidecar containing `keybinds` or `default_shell` should fail before launching Zellij and print a clear diagnostic naming the forbidden node.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if Zellij sidecar support requires a full native Zellij config merge framework.
- Stop if the implementation cannot fail clearly on dangerous ownership nodes.
- Stop if supporting a requested preference requires touching managed layout, managed keymaps, or plugin orchestration; create a separate bead instead.
- Stop if the validator grows into a broad Zellij schema or semantic keybinding system.

## Maintenance Notes

- This sidecar is intentionally stricter than Mars config override because Zellij config owns the Yazelix Next runtime spine.
- Future semantic key remaps should be a separate owner-scoped config surface, not arbitrary native `keybinds` in this file.
- Rejected direction: letting `~/.config/zellij/config.kdl` or a full user-owned Zellij config become the default for managed `yzn` sessions.
close_reason: Implemented guarded Zellij native config sidecar: yzn validates optional config.kdl, appends safe preferences into runtime config, rejects dangerous ownership nodes, documents the surface, updates LOC, and passes flake/package checks.

Installed yzn profile is refreshed.
